skill.name: video-inpainting
skill.description: Guide agents through RunComfy CLI routes for video region editing, object removal, watermark cleanup, restyling, and frame-by-frame inpainting workflows.
skill.summary: Selects RunComfy video inpainting routes for region edits across frames.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported critical and high matches, but review found no confirmed malicious intent. The Ruby backtick, Windows SAM, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance matches are false positives from Markdown text, model names, and SAM2 segmentation references. Real risk remains because the skill intentionally uses the runcomfy CLI, authenticates with a token, and sends user media or media URLs to RunComfy services.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Sends Media to Third-Party Service
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash(runcomfy *) and instructs agents to run RunComfy CLI commands. This is expected behavior, but it can upload or process user media through RunComfy Model API endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool and CLI examples are explicit, and the text states that the CLI posts to the Model API. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it creates external processing and privacy risk.

low_findings.title: Local Token File Documented
low_findings.description: The skill documents that runcomfy login stores an API token under the user's config directory with mode 0600. This is normal CLI behavior, but users should understand where credentials are stored.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and permission mode are directly documented. It is a transparency note rather than evidence of credential theft.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive-File and Crypto Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM findings refer to SAM2 segmentation, not the Windows SAM database. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance matches are text false positives with no executable code evidence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive Markdown about video workflows and prompt tips. I found no code that accesses Windows system files or uses weak cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Service Links
low_findings.description: The URL findings point to RunComfy documentation, catalog pages, and example input URLs. They do not show hidden telemetry or unrelated exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links are visible documentation and marketplace references. The only operational network behavior described is the intended RunComfy API route.

dangerous_patterns.title: Restricted External Command Allowlist
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill allows Bash(runcomfy *) commands. The restriction narrows scope, but command execution still depends on a locally installed CLI and user-provided inputs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Bash allowlist is explicit and scoped to runcomfy. The risk is bounded but real because it executes an external program.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound RunComfy Network Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill describes outbound API calls to RunComfy domains and generated result downloads. This is required for function but should be disclosed to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The outbound endpoint allowlist and Model API behavior are documented in the skill. No unrelated endpoint or covert network path was found.

content.value_statement: Video cleanup often needs consistent region edits across many frames. This skill selects RunComfy CLI routes for object removal, restyling, and frame-stack inpainting.
actual_capabilities: Selects Wan 2-7 Edit-Video for prompt-driven region edits.
Routes identity-sensitive restyles to Lucy Edit Restyle.
Suggests Seedream 4-0 Edit-Sequential for short frame stacks.
Provides RunComfy CLI examples for video edit jobs.
Explains when mask-driven ComfyUI workflows are more suitable.
Documents token handling, endpoint scope, and output size limits.
limitations: It depends on the separate runcomfy CLI and RunComfy account access.
Prompt-driven edits are not pixel-precise mask propagation.
Long clips can lose temporal consistency with frame-stack workflows.
User media or media URLs may be processed by RunComfy services.

use_cases.title: Remove Persistent Visual Clutter
use_cases.description: Clean a logo, wire, or background object that appears across many frames.

use_cases.title: Plan Region-Aware Restyles
use_cases.description: Choose a route for outfit, object, or surface changes that must follow motion.

use_cases.title: Compare CLI and ComfyUI Options
use_cases.description: Decide when a prompt-only endpoint is enough and when mask workflows are required.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Object Removal
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the visible object from this video. Preserve all other content and keep motion consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Watermark Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the watermark in the bottom-right corner across all frames. Match the background and avoid restyling the video.

prompt_templates.title: Identity-Stable Replacement
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the selected object with a similar item. Keep lighting, perspective, and subject identity stable.

prompt_templates.title: Route Selection Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this video edit goal and choose the best RunComfy route. Explain CLI steps, limitations, and when ComfyUI masking is needed.

output_examples.input: Remove a passing person from the background of a short clip.
output_examples.output: Recommended Wan 2-7 Edit-Video with a focused prompt that preserves the rest of the frame.

output_examples.input: Replace a product package while keeping hand motion natural.
output_examples.output: Suggested a region-aware restyle first, with frame extraction only if brand accuracy is required.

output_examples.input: Clean a small logo from a long production video.
output_examples.output: Flagged prompt-only editing as risky and recommended a mask-driven ComfyUI workflow for better consistency.

best_practices: Use one clear edit goal per RunComfy job.
Describe the target region with precise spatial language.
Review sensitive media policies before sending video URLs to external services.
anti_patterns: Do not combine many unrelated edits in one prompt.
Do not expect prompt-only endpoints to provide pixel-perfect masks.
Do not process private videos without explicit user approval.

faq.question: Does this skill edit videos locally?
faq.answer: No. It guides the runcomfy CLI, which uses RunComfy cloud services.

faq.question: Can it remove a watermark from every frame?
faq.answer: It can route that request, but quality depends on the clip and selected workflow.

faq.question: Does it support exact masks?
faq.answer: The CLI routes are prompt-driven. Pixel-precise masks need a ComfyUI workflow.

faq.question: What credentials are needed?
faq.answer: The runcomfy CLI needs login or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential media?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow may send media or media URLs to RunComfy services.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
